   127→   127→  </Step>
   128→   128→
   129→   129→  <Step>
   130→   130→    ### Add the client plugin
   131→   131→
   132→   132→    ```ts title="auth-client.ts"
   133→   133→    import { createAuthClient } from "better-auth/client"
   134→   134→    import { organizationClient } from "better-auth/client/plugins"
   135→   135→
   136→   136→    export const authClient = createAuthClient({
   137→   137→        plugins: [ // [!code highlight]
   138→   138→            organizationClient() // [!code highlight]
   139→   139→        ] // [!code highlight]
   140→   140→    })
   141→   141→    ```
   142→   142→  </Step>
   143→   143→</Steps>
   144→   144→
   145→   145→## Usage
   146→   146→
   147→   147→Once you've installed the plugin, you can start using the organization plugin to manage your organization's members and teams. The client plugin will provide you with methods under the `organization` namespace, and the server `api` will provide you with the necessary endpoints to manage your organization and give you an easier way to call the functions on your own backend.
   148→   148→
   149→   149→## Organization
   150→   150→
   151→   151→### Create an organization
   152→   152→
   153→   153→
   154→   154→### Client Side
   155→   155→
   156→   156→```ts
   157→   157→const { data, error } = await authClient.organization.create({
   158→   158→    name: My Organization,
   159→   159→    slug: my-org,
   160→   160→    logo: https://example.com/logo.png, // optional
   161→   161→    metadata, // optional
   162→   162→    userId: some_user_id, // optional
   163→   163→    keepCurrentActiveOrganization, // optional
   164→   164→});
   165→   165→```
   166→   166→
   167→   167→### Server Side
   168→   168→
   169→   169→```ts
   170→   170→const data = await auth.api.createOrganization({
   171→   171→    body: {
   172→   172→        name: My Organization,
   173→   173→        slug: my-org,
   174→   174→        logo: https://example.com/logo.png, // optional
   175→   175→        metadata, // optional
   176→   176→        userId: some_user_id, // optional
   177→   177→        keepCurrentActiveOrganization, // optional
   178→   178→    },
   179→   179→    // This endpoint requires session cookies.
   180→   180→    headers: await headers()
   181→   181→});
   182→   182→```
   183→   183→
   184→   184→### Type Definition
   185→   185→
   186→   186→```ts
   187→   187→type createOrganization = {
   188→   188→    /**
   189→   189→    * The organization name.
   190→   190→    */
   191→   191→    name: string = "My Organization"
   192→   192→    /**
   193→   193→    * The organization slug.
   194→   194→    */
   195→   195→    slug: string = "my-org"
   196→   196→    /**
   197→   197→    * The organization logo.
   198→   198→    */
   199→   199→    logo?: string = "https://example.com/logo.png"
   200→   200→    /**
   201→   201→    * The metadata of the organization.
   202→   202→    */
   203→   203→    metadata?: Record<string, any>
   204→   204→    /**
   205→   205→    * The user ID of the organization creator.
   206→   206→    * @serverOnly - This is ignored if session headers are provided.
   207→   207→    */
   208→   208→    userId?: string = "some_user_id"
   209→   209→    /**
   210→   210→    * Whether to keep the current active organization active after creating a new one.
   211→   211→    */
   212→   212→    keepCurrentActiveOrganization?: boolean = false
   213→   213→  
   214→   214→}
   215→   215→```
   216→   216→
   217→   217→
   218→   218→<Callout type="warn">
   219→   219→  **Mutually Exclusive Parameters**
   220→   220→
   221→   221→  The `userId` and session headers cannot be used together:
   222→   222→
   223→   223→  * **With session headers:** The organization is created for the authenticated session user. The `userId` field is **silently ignored**.
   224→   224→  * **Without session headers (Server-side only):** The organization is created for the user specified by `userId`.
   225→   225→
   226→   226→  **For Admins:** To create an organization on behalf of another user, you must make the API call server-side **without** passing session headers.
   227→   227→</Callout>
   228→   228→
   229→   229→#### Restrict who can create an organization
   230→   230→
   231→   231→By default, any user can create an organization. To restrict this, set the `allowUserToCreateOrganization` option to a function that returns a boolean, or directly to `true` or `false`.
   232→   232→
   233→   233→```ts title="auth.ts"
   234→   234→import { betterAuth } from "better-auth";
   235→   235→import { organization } from "better-auth/plugins";
   236→   236→
   237→   237→const auth = betterAuth({
   238→   238→  //...
   239→   239→  plugins: [
   240→   240→    organization({
   241→   241→      allowUserToCreateOrganization: async (user) => {
   242→   242→        // [!code highlight]
   243→   243→        const subscription = await getSubscription(user.id); // [!code highlight]
   244→   244→        return subscription.plan === "pro"; // [!code highlight]
   245→   245→      }, // [!code highlight]
   246→   246→    }),
   247→   247→  ],
   248→   248→});
   249→   249→```
   250→   250→
   251→   251→#### Check if organization slug is taken
   252→   252→
   253→   253→To check if an organization slug is taken or not you can use the `checkSlug` function provided by the client. The function takes an object with the following properties:
   254→   254→
   255→   255→
   256→   256→### Client Side
   257→   257→
   258→   258→```ts
   259→   259→const { data, error } = await authClient.organization.checkSlug({
   260→   260→    slug: my-org,
   261→   261→});
   262→   262→```
   263→   263→
   264→   264→### Server Side
   265→   265→
   266→   266→```ts
   267→   267→const data = await auth.api.checkOrganizationSlug({
   268→   268→    body: {
   269→   269→        slug: my-org,
   270→   270→    }
   271→   271→});
   272→   272→```
   273→   273→
   274→   274→### Type Definition
   275→   275→
   276→   276→```ts
   277→   277→type checkOrganizationSlug = {
   278→   278→      /**
   279→   279→       * The organization slug to check.  
   280→   280→       */
   281→   281→      slug: string = "my-org"
   282→   282→  
   283→   283→}
   284→   284→```
   285→   285→
   286→   286→
   287→   287→### Organization Hooks
   288→   288→
   289→   289→You can customize organization operations using hooks that run before and after various organization-related activities. Better Auth provides two ways to configure hooks:
   290→   290→
   291→   291→1. **Legacy organizationCreation hooks** (deprecated, use `organizationHooks` instead)
   292→   292→2. **Modern organizationHooks** (recommended) - provides comprehensive control over all organization-related activities
   293→   293→
   294→   294→#### Organization Creation and Management Hooks
   295→   295→
   296→   296→Control organization lifecycle operations:
   297→   297→
   298→   298→```ts title="auth.ts"
   299→   299→import { betterAuth } from "better-auth";
   300→   300→import { organization } from "better-auth/plugins";
   301→   301→
   302→   302→export const auth = betterAuth({
   303→   303→  plugins: [
   304→   304→    organization({
   305→   305→      organizationHooks: {
   306→   306→        // Organization creation hooks
   307→   307→        beforeCreateOrganization: async ({ organization, user }) => {
   308→   308→          // Run custom logic before organization is created
   309→   309→          // Optionally modify the organization data
   310→   310→          return {
   311→   311→            data: {
   312→   312→              ...organization,
   313→   313→              metadata: {
   314→   314→                customField: "value",
   315→   315→              },
   316→   316→            },
   317→   317→          };
   318→   318→        },
   319→   319→
   320→   320→        afterCreateOrganization: async ({ organization, member, user }) => {
   321→   321→          // Run custom logic after organization is created
   322→   322→          // e.g., create default resources, send notifications
   323→   323→          await setupDefaultResources(organization.id);
   324→   324→        },
   325→   325→
   326→   326→        // Organization update hooks
   327→   327→        beforeUpdateOrganization: async ({ organization, user, member }) => {
   328→   328→          // Validate updates, apply business rules
   329→   329→          return {
   330→   330→            data: {
   331→   331→              ...organization,
   332→   332→              name: organization.name?.toLowerCase(),
   333→   333→            },
   334→   334→          };
   335→   335→        },
   336→   336→
   337→   337→        afterUpdateOrganization: async ({ organization, user, member }) => {
   338→   338→          // Sync changes to external systems
   339→   339→          await syncOrganizationToExternalSystems(organization);
   340→   340→        },
   341→   341→      },
   342→   342→    }),
   343→   343→  ],
   344→   344→});
   345→   345→```
   346→   346→
   347→   347→<Callout type="info">
   348→   348→  The legacy `organizationCreation` hooks are still supported but deprecated.
   349→   349→  Use `organizationHooks.beforeCreateOrganization` and
   350→   350→  `organizationHooks.afterCreateOrganization` instead for new projects.
   351→   351→</Callout>
   352→   352→
   353→   353→#### Member Hooks
   354→   354→
   355→   355→Control member operations within organizations:
   356→   356→
   357→   357→```ts title="auth.ts"
   358→   358→import { betterAuth } from "better-auth";
   359→   359→import { organization } from "better-auth/plugins";
   360→   360→
   361→   361→export const auth = betterAuth({
   362→   362→  plugins: [
   363→   363→    organization({
   364→   364→      organizationHooks: {
   365→   365→        // Before a member is added to an organization
   366→   366→        beforeAddMember: async ({ member, user, organization }) => {
   367→   367→          // Custom validation or modification
   368→   368→          console.log(`Adding ${user.email} to ${organization.name}`);
   369→   369→
   370→   370→          // Optionally modify member data
   371→   371→          return {
   372→   372→            data: {
   373→   373→              ...member,
   374→   374→              role: "custom-role", // Override the role
   375→   375→            },
   376→   376→          };
   377→   377→        },
   378→   378→
   379→   379→        // After a member is added
   380→   380→        afterAddMember: async ({ member, user, organization }) => {
   381→   381→          // Send welcome email, create default resources, etc.
   382→   382→          await sendWelcomeEmail(user.email, organization.name);
   383→   383→        },
   384→   384→
   385→   385→        // Before a member is removed
   386→   386→        beforeRemoveMember: async ({ member, user, organization }) => {
   387→   387→          // Cleanup user's resources, send notification, etc.
   388→   388→          await cleanupUserResources(user.id, organization.id);
   389→   389→        },
   390→   390→
   391→   391→        // After a member is removed
   392→   392→        afterRemoveMember: async ({ member, user, organization }) => {
   393→   393→          await logMemberRemoval(user.id, organization.id);
   394→   394→        },
   395→   395→
   396→   396→        // Before updating a member's role
   397→   397→        beforeUpdateMemberRole: async ({
   398→   398→          member,
   399→   399→          newRole,
   400→   400→          user,
   401→   401→          organization,
   402→   402→        }) => {
   403→   403→          // Validate role change permissions
   404→   404→          if (newRole === "owner" && !hasOwnerUpgradePermission(user)) {
   405→   405→            throw new Error("Cannot upgrade to owner role");
   406→   406→          }
   407→   407→
   408→   408→          // Optionally modify the role
   409→   409→          return {
   410→   410→            data: {
   411→   411→              role: newRole,
   412→   412→            },
   413→   413→          };
   414→   414→        },
   415→   415→
   416→   416→        // After updating a member's role
   417→   417→        afterUpdateMemberRole: async ({
   418→   418→          member,
   419→   419→          previousRole,
   420→   420→          user,
   421→   421→          organization,
   422→   422→        }) => {
   423→   423→          await logRoleChange(user.id, previousRole, member.role);
   424→   424→        },
   425→   425→      },
   426→   426→    }),
   427→   427→  ],
   428→   428→});
   429→   429→```
   430→   430→
   431→   431→#### Invitation Hooks
   432→   432→
   433→   433→Control invitation lifecycle:
   434→   434→
   435→   435→```ts title="auth.ts"
   436→   436→export const auth = betterAuth({
   437→   437→  plugins: [
   438→   438→    organization({
   439→   439→      organizationHooks: {
   440→   440→        // Before creating an invitation
   441→   441→        beforeCreateInvitation: async ({
   442→   442→          invitation,
   443→   443→          inviter,
   444→   444→          organization,
   445→   445→        }) => {
   446→   446→          // Custom validation or expiration logic
   447→   447→          const customExpiration = new Date(
   448→   448→            Date.now() + 1000 * 60 * 60 * 24 * 7
   449→   449→          ); // 7 days
   450→   450→
   451→   451→          return {
   452→   452→            data: {
   453→   453→              ...invitation,
   454→   454→              expiresAt: customExpiration,
   455→   455→            },
   456→   456→          };
   457→   457→        },
   458→   458→
   459→   459→        // After creating an invitation
   460→   460→        afterCreateInvitation: async ({
   461→   461→          invitation,
   462→   462→          inviter,
   463→   463→          organization,
   464→   464→        }) => {
   465→   465→          // Send custom invitation email, track metrics, etc.
   466→   466→          await sendCustomInvitationEmail(invitation, organization);
   467→   467→        },
   468→   468→
   469→   469→        // Before accepting an invitation
   470→   470→        beforeAcceptInvitation: async ({ invitation, user, organization }) => {
   471→   471→          // Additional validation before acceptance
   472→   472→          await validateUserEligibility(user, organization);
   473→   473→        },
   474→   474→
   475→   475→        // After accepting an invitation
   476→   476→        afterAcceptInvitation: async ({
   477→   477→          invitation,
   478→   478→          member,
   479→   479→          user,
   480→   480→          organization,
   481→   481→        }) => {
   482→   482→          // Setup user account, assign default resources
   483→   483→          await setupNewMemberResources(user, organization);
   484→   484→        },
   485→   485→
   486→   486→        // Before/after rejecting invitations
   487→   487→        beforeRejectInvitation: async ({ invitation, user, organization }) => {
   488→   488→          // Log rejection reason, send notification to inviter
   489→   489→        },
   490→   490→
   491→   491→        afterRejectInvitation: async ({ invitation, user, organization }) => {
   492→   492→          await notifyInviterOfRejection(invitation.inviterId, user.email);
   493→   493→        },
   494→   494→
   495→   495→        // Before/after cancelling invitations
   496→   496→        beforeCancelInvitation: async ({
   497→   497→          invitation,
   498→   498→          cancelledBy,
   499→   499→          organization,
   500→   500→        }) => {

1→# Organization
     2→
     3→The organization plugin allows you to manage your organization's members and teams.

     7→Organizations simplifies user access and permissions management. Assign roles and permissions to streamline project management, team coordination, and partnerships.

   145→## Usage
   146→
   147→Once you've installed the plugin, you can start using the organization plugin to manage your organization's members and teams. The client plugin will provide you with methods under the `organization` namespace, and the server `api` will provide you with the necessary endpoints to manage your organization and give you an easier way to call the functions on your own backend.
   148→
   149→## Organization
   150→
   151→### Create an organization
   152→
   153→
   154→### Client Side
   155→
   156→```ts
   157→const { data, error } = await authClient.organization.create({
   158→    name: My Organization,
   159→    slug: my-org,
   160→    logo: https://example.com/logo.png, // optional
   161→    metadata, // optional
   162→    userId: some_user_id, // optional
   163→    keepCurrentActiveOrganization, // optional
   164→});
   165→```
   166→
   167→### Server Side
   168→
   169→```ts
   170→const data = await auth.api.createOrganization({
   171→    body: {
   172→        name: My Organization,
   173→        slug: my-org,
   174→        logo: https://example.com/logo.png, // optional
   175→        metadata, // optional
   176→        userId: some_user_id, // optional
   177→        keepCurrentActiveOrganization, // optional
   178→    },
   179→    // This endpoint requires session cookies.
   180→    headers: await headers()
   181→});
   182→```
   183→
   184→### Type Definition
   185→
   186→```ts
   187→type createOrganization = {
   188→    /**
   189→    * The organization name.
   190→    */
   191→    name: string = "My Organization"
   192→    /**
   193→    * The organization slug.
   194→    */
   195→    slug: string = "my-org"
   196→    /**
   197→    * The organization logo.
   198→    */
   199→    logo?: string = "https://example.com/logo.png"
   200→    /**
   201→    * The metadata of the organization.
   202→    */
   203→    metadata?: Record<string, any>
   204→    /**
   205→    * The user ID of the organization creator.
   206→    * @serverOnly - This is ignored if session headers are provided.
   207→    */
   208→    userId?: string = "some_user_id"
   209→    /**
   210→    * Whether to keep the current active organization active after creating a new one.
   211→    */
   212→    keepCurrentActiveOrganization?: boolean = false
   213→  
   214→}
   215→```
   216→
   217→
   218→<Callout type="warn">
   219→  **Mutually Exclusive Parameters**
   220→
   221→  The `userId` and session headers cannot be used together:
   222→
   223→  * **With session headers:** The organization is created for the authenticated session user. The `userId` field is **silently ignored**.
   224→  * **Without session headers (Server-side only):** The organization is created for the user specified by `userId`.
   225→
   226→  **For Admins:** To create an organization on behalf of another user, you must make the API call server-side **without** passing session headers.
   227→</Callout>
   228→
   229→#### Restrict who can create an organization
   230→
   231→By default, any user can create an organization. To restrict this, set the `allowUserToCreateOrganization` option to a function that returns a boolean, or directly to `true` or `false`.
   232→
   233→```ts title="auth.ts"
   234→import { betterAuth } from "better-auth";
   235→import { organization } from "better-auth/plugins";
   236→
   237→const auth = betterAuth({
   238→  //...
   239→  plugins: [
   240→    organization({
   241→      allowUserToCreateOrganization: async (user) => {
   242→        // [!code highlight]
   243→        const subscription = await getSubscription(user.id); // [!code highlight]
   244→        return subscription.plan === "pro"; // [!code highlight]
   245→      }, // [!code highlight]
   246→    }),
   247→  ],
   248→});
   249→```
   250→
   251→#### Check if organization slug is taken
   252→
   253→To check if an organization slug is taken or not you can use the `checkSlug` function provided by the client. The function takes an object with the following properties:
   254→
   255→
   256→### Client Side
   257→
   258→```ts
   259→const { data, error } = await authClient.organization.checkSlug({
   260→    slug: my-org,
   261→});
   262→```
   263→
   264→### Server Side
   265→
   266→```ts
   267→const data = await auth.api.checkOrganizationSlug({
   268→    body: {
   269→        slug: my-org,
   270→    }
   271→});
   272→```
   273→
   274→### Type Definition
   275→
   276→```ts
   277→type checkOrganizationSlug = {
   278→      /**
   279→       * The organization slug to check.  
   280→       */
   281→      slug: string = "my-org"
   282→  
   283→}
   284→```
   285→
   286→
   287→### Organization Hooks
   288→
   289→You can customize organization operations using hooks that run before and after various organization-related activities. Better Auth provides two ways to configure hooks:
   290→
   291→1. **Legacy organizationCreation hooks** (deprecated, use `organizationHooks` instead)
   292→2. **Modern organizationHooks** (recommended) - provides comprehensive control over all organization-related activities
   293→
   294→#### Organization Creation and Management Hooks
   295→
   296→Control organization lifecycle operations:
   297→
   298→```ts title="auth.ts"
   299→import { betterAuth } from "better-auth";
   300→import { organization } from "better-auth/plugins";
   301→
   302→export const auth = betterAuth({
   303→  plugins: [
   304→    organization({
   305→      organizationHooks: {
   306→        // Organization creation hooks
   307→        beforeCreateOrganization: async ({ organization, user }) => {
   308→          // Run custom logic before organization is created
   309→          // Optionally modify the organization data
   310→          return {
   311→            data: {
   312→              ...organization,
   313→              metadata: {
   314→                customField: "value",
   315→              },
   316→            },
   317→          };
   318→        },
   319→
   320→        afterCreateOrganization: async ({ organization, member, user }) => {
   321→          // Run custom logic after organization is created
   322→          // e.g., create default resources, send notifications
   323→          await setupDefaultResources(organization.id);
   324→        },
   325→
   326→        // Organization update hooks
   327→        beforeUpdateOrganization: async ({ organization, user, member }) => {
   328→          // Validate updates, apply business rules
   329→          return {
   330→            data: {
   331→              ...organization,
   332→              name: organization.name?.toLowerCase(),
   333→            },
   334→          };
   335→        },
   336→
   337→        afterUpdateOrganization: async ({ organization, user, member }) => {
   338→          // Sync changes to external systems
   339→          await syncOrganizationToExternalSystems(organization);
   340→        },
   341→      },
   342→    }),
   343→  ],
   344→});
   345→```
   346→
   347→<Callout type="info">
   348→  The legacy `organizationCreation` hooks are still supported but deprecated.
   349→  Use `organizationHooks.beforeCreateOrganization` and
   350→  `organizationHooks.afterCreateOrganization` instead for new projects.
   351→</Callout>
   352→
   353→#### Member Hooks
   354→
   355→Control member operations within organizations:
   356→
   357→```ts title="auth.ts"
   358→import { betterAuth } from "better-auth";
   359→import { organization } from "better-auth/plugins";
   360→
   361→export const auth = betterAuth({
   362→  plugins: [
   363→    organization({
   364→      organizationHooks: {
   365→        // Before a member is added to an organization
   366→        beforeAddMember: async ({ member, user, organization }) => {
   367→          // Custom validation or modification
   368→          console.log(`Adding ${user.email} to ${organization.name}`);
   369→
   370→          // Optionally modify member data
   371→          return {
   372→            data: {
   373→              ...member,
   374→              role: "custom-role", // Override the role
   375→            },
   376→          };
   377→        },
   378→
   379→        // After a member is added
   380→        afterAddMember: async ({ member, user, organization }) => {
   381→          // Send welcome email, create default resources, etc.
   382→          await sendWelcomeEmail(user.email, organization.name);
   383→        },
   384→
   385→        // Before a member is removed
   386→        beforeRemoveMember: async ({ member, user, organization }) => {
   387→          // Cleanup user's resources, send notification, etc.
   388→          await cleanupUserResources(user.id, organization.id);
   389→        },
   390→
   391→        // After a member is removed
   392→        afterRemoveMember: async ({ member, user, organization }) => {
   393→          await logMemberRemoval(user.id, organization.id);
   394→        },
   395→
   396→        // Before updating a member's role
   397→        beforeUpdateMemberRole: async ({
   398→          member,
   399→          newRole,
   400→          user,
   401→          organization,
   402→        }) => {
   403→          // Validate role change permissions
   404→          if (newRole === "owner" && !hasOwnerUpgradePermission(user)) {
   405→            throw new Error("Cannot upgrade to owner role");
   406→          }
   407→
   408→          // Optionally modify the role
   409→          return {
   410→            data: {
   411→              role: newRole,
   412→            },
   413→          };
   414→        },
   415→
   416→        // After updating a member's role
   417→        afterUpdateMemberRole: async ({
   418→          member,
   419→          previousRole,
   420→          user,
   421→          organization,
   422→        }) => {
   423→          await logRoleChange(user.id, previousRole, member.role);
   424→        },
   425→      },
   426→    }),
   427→  ],
   428→});
   429→```
   430→
   431→#### Invitation Hooks
   432→
   433→Control invitation lifecycle:
   434→
   435→```ts title="auth.ts"
   436→export const auth = betterAuth({
   437→  plugins: [
   438→    organization({
   439→      organizationHooks: {
   440→        // Before creating an invitation
   441→        beforeCreateInvitation: async ({
   442→          invitation,
   443→          inviter,
   444→          organization,
   445→        }) => {
   446→          // Custom validation or expiration logic
   447→          const customExpiration = new Date(
   448→            Date.now() + 1000 * 60 * 60 * 24 * 7
   449→          ); // 7 days
   450→
   451→          return {
   452→            data: {
   453→              ...invitation,
   454→              expiresAt: customExpiration,
   455→            },
   456→          };
   457→        },
   458→
   459→        // After creating an invitation
   460→        afterCreateInvitation: async ({
   461→          invitation,
   462→          inviter,
   463→          organization,
   464→        }) => {
   465→          // Send custom invitation email, track metrics, etc.
   466→          await sendCustomInvitationEmail(invitation, organization);
   467→        },
   468→
   469→        // Before accepting an invitation
   470→        beforeAcceptInvitation: async ({ invitation, user, organization }) => {
   471→          // Additional validation before acceptance
   472→          await validateUserEligibility(user, organization);
   473→        },
   474→
   475→        // After accepting an invitation
   476→        afterAcceptInvitation: async ({
   477→          invitation,
   478→          member,
   479→          user,
   480→          organization,
   481→        }) => {
   482→          // Setup user account, assign default resources
   483→          await setupNewMemberResources(user, organization);
   484→        },
   485→
   486→        // Before/after rejecting invitations
   487→        beforeRejectInvitation: async ({ invitation, user, organization }) => {
   488→          // Log rejection reason, send notification to inviter
   489→        },
   490→
   491→        afterRejectInvitation: async ({ invitation, user, organization }) => {
   492→          await notifyInviterOfRejection(invitation.inviterId, user.email);
   493→        },
   494→
   495→        // Before/after cancelling invitations
   496→        beforeCancelInvitation: async ({
   497→          invitation,
   498→          cancelledBy,
   499→          organization,
   500→        }) => {

content.thinking:
Now I have the full picture from the docs. Let me compare what better-auth expects vs what the Drizzle schema has:

**Organization table** (docs):
- id (string, PK) ✅
- name (string) ✅
- slug (string) ✅
- logo (string, optional) ✅
- metadata (string, optional) ✅
- createdAt (Date) ✅

**Member table** (docs):
- id (string, PK) ✅
- userId (string, FK) ✅
- organizationId (string, FK) ✅


- role (string) ✅
- createdAt (Date) ✅

**Invitation table** (docs):
- id (string, PK) ✅
- email (string) ✅
- inviterId (string, FK) ✅
- organizationId (string, FK) ✅
- role (string) ✅
- status (string) ✅
- createdAt (Date) ❌ MISSING in Drizzle schema
- expiresAt (Date) ✅

The Session table also needs an activeOrganizationId field added as an optional string.

Now I'm looking at the teamId field on the invitation table. The docs say it's only required if teams are enabled, but the adapter code appears to always write a teamId value—either joining the team IDs or setting it to null. Let me trace through the adapter code to see if this field is conditionally included or always present...

Examining the createInvitation function...

So teamId gets set in the data object regardless of whether teams are actually enabled, which means the Drizzle adapter's field validation will fail if teamId isn't in the schema. There's something subtle happening here with how the field is being handled.

Looking at the adapter code for invitation acceptance, it seems like activeTeamId might also be getting written to sessions, but that's only relevant if teams are enabled. The core issue I need to focus on is that both createdAt and teamId are missing from the invitation table schema, and those are what's causing the validation failures.

content.text:
Good, I now have the full picture from the docs. Let me compare the official better-auth schema with the current Drizzle schema to find all gaps.

**Invitation table** — better-auth docs require:
| Field | Type | Drizzle schema | Status |
|-------|------|---------------|--------|
| id | string (PK) | ✅ | OK |
| email | string | ✅ | OK |
| inviterId | string (FK) | ✅ | OK |
| organizationId | string (FK) | ✅ | OK |
| role | string | ✅ | OK |
| status | string | ✅ | OK |
| **createdAt** | Date | ❌ | **MISSING** |
| expiresAt | Date | ✅ | OK |

And the adapter code **always** writes `teamId` (set to `null` when teams aren't enabled) at `adapter.mjs:556`, so it also needs to be in the schema even though we don't use teams.

The other tables (`organization`, `member`, `session`) are already aligned. Let me fix the Drizzle schema and generate the migration.

input.new_string:
export const invitation = pgTable("invitation", {
	id: text("id").primaryKey(),
	organizationId: text("organization_id")
		.notNull()
		.references(() => organization.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
	email: text("email").notNull(),
	role: text("role"),
	status: text("status").notNull().default("pending"),
	expiresAt: timestamp("expires_at", {
		withTimezone: true,
		mode: "date",
	}).notNull(),
	inviterId: text("inviter_id")
		.notNull()
		.references(() => user.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
	createdAt: timestamp("created_at", { withTimezone: true, mode: "date" })
		.defaultNow()
		.notNull(),
	teamId: text("team_id"),
});

content.text:
Clean and minimal. Here's a summary of the changes:

**What was done:**

1. **Root cause confirmed**: The `invitation` Drizzle table was missing `createdAt` and `teamId` columns that better-auth's organization plugin always writes during `createInvitation`. The Drizzle adapter's `checkMissingFields()` throws a `BetterAuthError` before the INSERT, so no invitation is ever created.

2. **Fix applied** — `packages/sql-schema/src/auth-schema.ts`:
   - Added `createdAt` (timestamp with timezone, default now, not null) — required by better-auth's invitation schema
   - Added `teamId` (text, nullable) — the adapter always sets this (to `null` when teams aren't enabled)

3. **Migration generated** — `packages/sql-schema/db/migrations/0003_clean_franklin_storm.sql`:
   ```sql
   ALTER TABLE "invitation" ADD COLUMN "created_at" timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now() NOT NULL;
   ALTER TABLE "invitation" ADD COLUMN "team_id" text;
   ```

**What still needs to happen:**
- This migration needs to be applied to both CI and production Postgres databases (via `bunx drizzle-kit migrate` with the appropriate connection strings)
- After deploying, the full invite flow should work end-to-end: send invite → copy link → recipient visits link → login → accept → member created

input.prompt:
I need to understand the test infrastructure and database migration setup in this project to plan an e2e migration test. Find and analyze:

1. **Existing test patterns**: How do tests in `apps/api/src/__tests__/` and `packages/ch-schema/src/__tests__/` set up and tear down databases? Do any tests spin up Postgres?

2. **Docker Compose setup**: Read `docker-compose.yml` or similar — how are local Postgres and ClickHouse configured?

3. **Drizzle migration setup**: Read `packages/sql-schema/drizzle.config.ts` — how are migrations applied? What's the connection config?

4. **CI test configuration**: How does `bun run verify` run tests? Does it use Docker? Is there a CI Postgres database?

5. **The `scripts/dev-local.sh`** script — how does it set up local infra?

6. **Any existing migration test patterns** — search for files with "migration" or "migrate" in test directories.

Read all relevant files and report back with details about the infrastructure.

content.text:
Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me compile the full analysis.

---

## Analysis: Test Infrastructure and Database Migration Setup

### Overview

The project has three test suites (`apps/api`, `apps/cli`, `packages/ch-schema`) that all use `bun:test`. Tests that need ClickHouse connect directly to a remote ClickHouse instance via environment variables (injected by Doppler in CI). Tests that need Postgres spin up a real API server subprocess that connects to Postgres via `PG_CONNECTION_STRING`. There are no migration-specific tests anywhere in the codebase. Local development uses Docker Compose for both Postgres and ClickHouse, while CI uses remote hosted databases.

---

### 1. Existing Test Patterns

#### `apps/api/src/__tests__/ingest.test.ts`

This file contains two kinds of tests:

**Pure unit tests (no database)** -- `buildSessionRow` and `extractTimestampRange` (lines 29-164). These test the data transformation functions that convert `IngestSessionInput` into `RudelClaudeSessionsRow`. They have zero infrastructure dependencies.

**ClickHouse integration test** (lines 232-270). This test:
- Creates a `ClickHouseExecutor` at lines 168-174 using env vars (`CLICKHOUSE_URL`, `CLICKHOUSE_USERNAME`/`CLICKHOUSE_USER`, `CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD`) with fallback to `http://localhost:8123`
- Wraps the executor's `insert` method (lines 179-187) to use `execute()` with `FORMAT JSONEachRow` and `SETTINGS async_insert=0` instead of the native `insert()` method
- Generates a unique test ID with `api_test_${Date.now()}_${random}` at line 166
- Uses a `waitForRow` polling function (lines 196-230) that retries every 2 seconds for up to 30 seconds
- Implements insert retry logic with exponential backoff (lines 248-260, up to 5 attempts)
- Cleans up via fire-and-forget `DELETE` in `afterAll` at lines 189-194
- Has a 120-second timeout on the integration test (line 269)

No Postgres is involved. No Docker is started. No database setup or teardown.

#### `packages/ch-schema/src/__tests__/schemas.test.ts`

Pure unit tests (lines 1-53). Tests that the generated Zod schema `RudelClaudeSessionsRowSchema` correctly validates row objects. No database connection.

#### `packages/ch-schema/src/__tests__/ingest.test.ts`

ClickHouse integration test (lines 1-127). Nearly identical pattern to the API ingest test:
- Creates a `ClickHouseExecutor` from `@chkit/clickhouse` at lines 8-14
- Same executor wrapping pattern with `FORMAT JSONEachRow` and retry logic (lines 19-38)
- Same `waitForQuery` polling helper (lines 41-57)
- Adds an `insertWithRetry` helper (lines 59-77) that combines insert + query verification
- Tests `ingestRudelClaudeSessions` generated function (line 105)
- Tests validation rejection of bad data (lines 117-125)
- Fire-and-forget `DELETE` cleanup in `afterAll` (lines 79-83)
- 120-second timeout (line 115)

#### `apps/cli/src/__tests__/upload.integration.test.ts`

File-system-only integration tests (lines 1-337). Creates temp directories, writes `.jsonl` files, tests session resolution, transcript reading, and subagent reading. No database connections. Uses `beforeAll`/`afterAll` for temp directory lifecycle (lines 43-49).

#### `apps/cli/src/__tests__/api-upload.integration.test.ts`

**This is the most complex test -- it spins up a real API server with Postgres.** Setup at lines 24-35:
- Creates a temp directory
- Calls `startTestServer()` which spawns the Bun API server as a subprocess
- Calls `signUpTestUser()` to create an auth user and get a bearer token

The test server helper is at `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai/apps/cli/src/__tests__/helpers/bun-server.ts`:
- `startTestServer()` (lines 18-63) spawns `bun apps/api/src/index.ts` with `PORT=0` and env overrides:
  - `PG_CONNECTION_STRING` is **not set** by the helper -- it inherits from the parent process environment
  - `BETTER_AUTH_SECRET` is set to `"test-secret-for-integration-tests"` (line 23)
  - `APP_URL` and `ALLOWED_ORIGIN` are set to `"http://localhost"` (lines 22, 24)
- Parses the actual port from stdout by watching for the "listening on" message (lines 89-129)
- `signUpTestUser()` (lines 156-186) sends a POST to `/api/auth/sign-up/email` and extracts the session token
- `ensureAlive()` (lines 47-59) can restart the server if Bun's test runner killed the subprocess

The tests use `beforeEach` (line 40) to call `ensureAlive()` before each test.

#### `apps/cli/src/__tests__/auth.e2e.test.ts`

Same `startTestServer()` + `signUpTestUser()` pattern (lines 26-33). Tests the full CLI login/logout/whoami flow by spawning CLI subprocesses with a custom `RUDEL_CONFIG_DIR`.

**Key observation: No test spins up its own Postgres.** The CLI e2e tests rely on `PG_CONNECTION_STRING` being available in the environment (via Doppler CI config or manual setup). The API server subprocess inherits this from the parent process.

---

### 2. Docker Compose Setup

**File**: `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai/docker-compose.yml`

Two services:

**Postgres** (lines 2-12):
- Image: `postgres:16-alpine`
- Port: `5432:5432`
- Credentials: `postgres/postgres`, database `rudel`
- Named volume: `postgres_data`
- Healthcheck: `pg_isready -U postgres` every 5s, 3 retries

**ClickHouse** (lines 14-31):
- Image: `clickhouse/clickhouse-server:latest`
- Port: `8123:8123` (HTTP interface only)
- Password: `clickhouse` (username defaults to `default`)
- Named volume: `clickhouse_data`
- **Init script**: mounts `./scripts/init-clickhouse-local.sql` to `/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init.sql`
- Healthcheck: `clickhouse-client --password clickhouse --query "SELECT 1"` every 5s

**ClickHouse init script** (`/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai/scripts/init-clickhouse-local.sql`):
- Creates database `rudel` (line 5)
- Creates `rudel.claude_sessions` table (lines 7-34) using `ReplacingMergeTree` (not `SharedReplacingMergeTree` as in cloud)
- Creates `rudel.session_analytics` table (lines 36-81) with computed analytics columns
- Creates `rudel.session_analytics_mv` materialized view (lines 83-125) that populates `session_analytics` from `claude_sessions` inserts

This is a local-only adaptation of the cloud schema. The comment on line 3 notes it's "Derived from packages/ch-schema/chx/migrations/20260220190421_auto.sql" but uses `ReplacingMergeTree` instead of `SharedReplacingMergeTree` and omits `storage_policy = 's3'`.

---

### 3. Drizzle Migration Setup

**Config**: `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai/packages/sql-schema/drizzle.config.ts`

```typescript
export default defineConfig({
    schema: "./src/auth-schema.ts",
    out: "./db/migrations",
    dialect: "postgresql",
});
```

- Schema source: `./src/auth-schema.ts` (lines 1-7)
- Migration output: `./db/migrations`
- No connection string in config -- Drizzle Kit generates SQL files offline

**Migration runner**: `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai/packages/sql-schema/src/migrate.ts`

- Reads `PG_CONNECTION_STRING` from env (line 6), throws if missing (line 8)
- Creates a single-connection postgres client (line 11: `max: 1`)
- Creates a drizzle instance and calls `migrate(db, { migrationsFolder })` (line 17)
- The `migrationsFolder` resolves to `packages/sql-schema/db/migrations` (line 14)
- Closes the connection after migration (line 20)

**Migration files** (4 total):

| File | Content |
|------|---------|
| `0000_supreme_texas_twister.sql` | Creates `account`, `session`, `user`, `verification` tables + FK constraints |
| `0001_flippant_sharon_ventura.sql` | Creates `invitation`, `member`, `organization` tables + adds `active_organization_id` to `session` + FK constraints |
| `0002_migrate-users-to-orgs.sql` | Data migration: creates personal orgs for existing users, owner memberships, sets `active_organization_id` |
| `0003_clean_franklin_storm.sql` | Adds `created_at` and `team_id` columns to `invitation` table |

**Migration journal**: `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai/packages/sql-schema/db/migrations/meta/_journal.json` tracks all 4 entries with timestamps and breakpoint settings.

**Package scripts** for running migrations:
- `bun run migrate` -- runs `src/migrate.ts` (requires `PG_CONNECTION_STRING` in env)
- `bun run migrate:ci` -- wraps with `doppler run --project rudel --config ci`
- `bun run migrate:prd` -- wraps with `doppler run --project rudel --config prd`

**The API server does NOT run migrations on startup.** The `db.ts` module (lines 1-11) only creates a drizzle client; it does not call `migrate()`. better-auth uses the drizzle adapter but does not auto-create tables either. Migrations must be run separately before the server starts. The `dev-local.sh` script handles this explicitly.

---

### 4. CI Test Configuration

**Workflow file**: `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai/.github/workflows/ci.yml`

The `verify` job (lines 10-43):
1. Runs on `ubuntu-latest` (line 11)
2. Checks out code, sets up Bun 1.3.5, caches `~/.bun/install/cache`
3. Installs with `bun install --frozen-lockfile`
4. Verifies lockfile unchanged

content.text:
Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me compile the full analysis.

---

## Analysis: Test Infrastructure and Database Migration Setup

### Overview

The project has three test suites (`apps/api`, `apps/cli`, `packages/ch-schema`) that all use `bun:test`. Tests that need ClickHouse connect directly to a remote ClickHouse instance via environment variables (injected by Doppler in CI). Tests that need Postgres spin up a real API server subprocess that connects to Postgres via `PG_CONNECTION_STRING`. There are no migration-specific tests anywhere in the codebase. Local development uses Docker Compose for both Postgres and ClickHouse, while CI uses remote hosted databases.

---

### 1. Existing Test Patterns

#### `apps/api/src/__tests__/ingest.test.ts`

This file contains two kinds of tests:

**Pure unit tests (no database)** -- `buildSessionRow` and `extractTimestampRange` (lines 29-164). These test the data transformation functions that convert `IngestSessionInput` into `RudelClaudeSessionsRow`. They have zero infrastructure dependencies.

**ClickHouse integration test** (lines 232-270). This test:
- Creates a `ClickHouseExecutor` at lines 168-174 using env vars (`CLICKHOUSE_URL`, `CLICKHOUSE_USERNAME`/`CLICKHOUSE_USER`, `CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD`) with fallback to `http://localhost:8123`
- Wraps the executor's `insert` method (lines 179-187) to use `execute()` with `FORMAT JSONEachRow` and `SETTINGS async_insert=0` instead of the native `insert()` method
- Generates a unique test ID with `api_test_${Date.now()}_${random}` at line 166
- Uses a `waitForRow` polling function (lines 196-230) that retries every 2 seconds for up to 30 seconds
- Implements insert retry logic with exponential backoff (lines 248-260, up to 5 attempts)
- Cleans up via fire-and-forget `DELETE` in `afterAll` at lines 189-194
- Has a 120-second timeout on the integration test (line 269)

No Postgres is involved. No Docker is started. No database setup or teardown.

#### `packages/ch-schema/src/__tests__/schemas.test.ts`

Pure unit tests (lines 1-53). Tests that the generated Zod schema `RudelClaudeSessionsRowSchema` correctly validates row objects. No database connection.

#### `packages/ch-schema/src/__tests__/ingest.test.ts`

ClickHouse integration test (lines 1-127). Nearly identical pattern to the API ingest test:
- Creates a `ClickHouseExecutor` from `@chkit/clickhouse` at lines 8-14
- Same executor wrapping pattern with `FORMAT JSONEachRow` and retry logic (lines 19-38)
- Same `waitForQuery` polling helper (lines 41-57)
- Adds an `insertWithRetry` helper (lines 59-77) that combines insert + query verification
- Tests `ingestRudelClaudeSessions` generated function (line 105)
- Tests validation rejection of bad data (lines 117-125)
- Fire-and-forget `DELETE` cleanup in `afterAll` (lines 79-83)
- 120-second timeout (line 115)

#### `apps/cli/src/__tests__/upload.integration.test.ts`

File-system-only integration tests (lines 1-337). Creates temp directories, writes `.jsonl` files, tests session resolution, transcript reading, and subagent reading. No database connections. Uses `beforeAll`/`afterAll` for temp directory lifecycle (lines 43-49).

#### `apps/cli/src/__tests__/api-upload.integration.test.ts`

**This is the most complex test -- it spins up a real API server with Postgres.** Setup at lines 24-35:
- Creates a temp directory
- Calls `startTestServer()` which spawns the Bun API server as a subprocess
- Calls `signUpTestUser()` to create an auth user and get a bearer token

The test server helper is at `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai/apps/cli/src/__tests__/helpers/bun-server.ts`:
- `startTestServer()` (lines 18-63) spawns `bun apps/api/src/index.ts` with `PORT=0` and env overrides:
  - `PG_CONNECTION_STRING` is **not set** by the helper -- it inherits from the parent process environment
  - `BETTER_AUTH_SECRET` is set to `"test-secret-for-integration-tests"` (line 23)
  - `APP_URL` and `ALLOWED_ORIGIN` are set to `"http://localhost"` (lines 22, 24)
- Parses the actual port from stdout by watching for the "listening on" message (lines 89-129)
- `signUpTestUser()` (lines 156-186) sends a POST to `/api/auth/sign-up/email` and extracts the session token
- `ensureAlive()` (lines 47-59) can restart the server if Bun's test runner killed the subprocess

The tests use `beforeEach` (line 40) to call `ensureAlive()` before each test.

#### `apps/cli/src/__tests__/auth.e2e.test.ts`

Same `startTestServer()` + `signUpTestUser()` pattern (lines 26-33). Tests the full CLI login/logout/whoami flow by spawning CLI subprocesses with a custom `RUDEL_CONFIG_DIR`.

**Key observation: No test spins up its own Postgres.** The CLI e2e tests rely on `PG_CONNECTION_STRING` being available in the environment (via Doppler CI config or manual setup). The API server subprocess inherits this from the parent process.

---

### 2. Docker Compose Setup

**File**: `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai/docker-compose.yml`

Two services:

**Postgres** (lines 2-12):
- Image: `postgres:16-alpine`
- Port: `5432:5432`
- Credentials: `postgres/postgres`, database `rudel`
- Named volume: `postgres_data`
- Healthcheck: `pg_isready -U postgres` every 5s, 3 retries

**ClickHouse** (lines 14-31):
- Image: `clickhouse/clickhouse-server:latest`
- Port: `8123:8123` (HTTP interface only)
- Password: `clickhouse` (username defaults to `default`)
- Named volume: `clickhouse_data`
- **Init script**: mounts `./scripts/init-clickhouse-local.sql` to `/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init.sql`
- Healthcheck: `clickhouse-client --password clickhouse --query "SELECT 1"` every 5s

**ClickHouse init script** (`/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai/scripts/init-clickhouse-local.sql`):
- Creates database `rudel` (line 5)
- Creates `rudel.claude_sessions` table (lines 7-34) using `ReplacingMergeTree` (not `SharedReplacingMergeTree` as in cloud)
- Creates `rudel.session_analytics` table (lines 36-81) with computed analytics columns
- Creates `rudel.session_analytics_mv` materialized view (lines 83-125) that populates `session_analytics` from `claude_sessions` inserts

This is a local-only adaptation of the cloud schema. The comment on line 3 notes it's "Derived from packages/ch-schema/chx/migrations/20260220190421_auto.sql" but uses `ReplacingMergeTree` instead of `SharedReplacingMergeTree` and omits `storage_policy = 's3'`.

---

### 3. Drizzle Migration Setup

**Config**: `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai/packages/sql-schema/drizzle.config.ts`

```typescript
export default defineConfig({
    schema: "./src/auth-schema.ts",
    out: "./db/migrations",
    dialect: "postgresql",
});
```

- Schema source: `./src/auth-schema.ts` (lines 1-7)
- Migration output: `./db/migrations`
- No connection string in config -- Drizzle Kit generates SQL files offline

**Migration runner**: `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai/packages/sql-schema/src/migrate.ts`

- Reads `PG_CONNECTION_STRING` from env (line 6), throws if missing (line 8)
- Creates a single-connection postgres client (line 11: `max: 1`)
- Creates a drizzle instance and calls `migrate(db, { migrationsFolder })` (line 17)
- The `migrationsFolder` resolves to `packages/sql-schema/db/migrations` (line 14)
- Closes the connection after migration (line 20)

**Migration files** (4 total):

| File | Content |
|------|---------|
| `0000_supreme_texas_twister.sql` | Creates `account`, `session`, `user`, `verification` tables + FK constraints |
| `0001_flippant_sharon_ventura.sql` | Creates `invitation`, `member`, `organization` tables + adds `active_organization_id` to `session` + FK constraints |
| `0002_migrate-users-to-orgs.sql` | Data migration: creates personal orgs for existing users, owner memberships, sets `active_organization_id` |
| `0003_clean_franklin_storm.sql` | Adds `created_at` and `team_id` columns to `invitation` table |

**Migration journal**: `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai/packages/sql-schema/db/migrations/meta/_journal.json` tracks all 4 entries with timestamps and breakpoint settings.

**Package scripts** for running migrations:
- `bun run migrate` -- runs `src/migrate.ts` (requires `PG_CONNECTION_STRING` in env)
- `bun run migrate:ci` -- wraps with `doppler run --project rudel --config ci`
- `bun run migrate:prd` -- wraps with `doppler run --project rudel --config prd`

**The API server does NOT run migrations on startup.** The `db.ts` module (lines 1-11) only creates a drizzle client; it does not call `migrate()`. better-auth uses the drizzle adapter but does not auto-create tables either. Migrations must be run separately before the server starts. The `dev-local.sh` script handles this explicitly.

---

### 4. CI Test Configuration

**Workflow file**: `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai/.github/workflows/ci.yml`

The `verify` job (lines 10-43):
1. Runs on `ubuntu-latest` (line 11)
2. Checks out code, sets up Bun 1.3.5, caches `~/.bun/install/cache`
3. Installs with `bun install --frozen-lockfile`
4. Verifies lockfile unchanged
5. Runs: `bunx turbo run lint check-types test build` (line 36)
6. Injects 5 secrets as env vars (lines 37-42): `PG_CONNECTION_STRING`, `CLICKHOUSE_URL`, `CLICKHOUSE_USERNAME`, `CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD`, `CLICKHOUSE_DB`

**No Docker is used in CI.** There are no `docker compose` steps or service containers in the CI workflow. All database connections go to remote hosted instances (provided via GitHub secrets from the Doppler `ci` config).

**Local `bun run verify`** (root `package.json` line 13):
```
doppler run --project rudel --config ci -- turbo run lint check-types test build
```
This injects the CI Doppler config secrets into all turbo tasks. The `ci` Doppler config provides connection strings to the CI ClickHouse and Postgres instances.

**Turbo task configuration**:
- Root `turbo.json` (line 22): `"test": {}` -- no dependencies, no caching
- `apps/api/turbo.json`, `apps/cli/turbo.json`, `packages/ch-schema/turbo.json` all override `test` to pass through ClickHouse env vars
- Global passthrough in root `turbo.json` (lines 4-10): `PG_CONNECTION_STRING`, `CLICKHOUSE_URL`, `CLICKHOUSE_USERNAME`, `CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD`, `CLICKHOUSE_DB`

**Test commands per package**:
- `apps/api`: `bun test` (package.json line 10)
- `apps/cli`: `bun test` (package.json line 19)
- `packages/ch-schema`: `bun test` (package.json line 11)
- `packages/sql-schema`: no `test` script -- it has no tests

---

### 5. `scripts/dev-local.sh`

**File**: `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai/scripts/dev-local.sh`

Three phases:

**Phase 1 -- Docker infrastructure** (line 5):
```bash
docker compose up -d --wait
```
Starts both Postgres and ClickHouse containers using the `docker-compose.yml` at the repo root. The `--wait` flag blocks until healthchecks pass.

**Phase 2 -- Postgres migrations** (lines 8-9):


content.thinking:
Now I have a thorough understanding of the test infrastructure. Let me write a plan for an e2e migration test.

The key insights are:
1. No migration tests exist yet
2. Tests use real databases (CI or local Docker)
3. The CLI e2e tests already have a pattern for spawning an API server subprocess that connects to Postgres
4. Postgres is available via Docker Compose locally and via Doppler CI config in CI
5. Drizzle tracks applied migrations in `__drizzle_migrations` table

Let me think about the approaches:

**Approach A: Docker-based isolated test**
- Spin up a fresh Postgres container just for the test


- Apply migrations 0000-0002, insert invitation-related test data, apply migration 0003, then verify the data and schema are correct
- Pros: Fully isolated, reproducible, no shared state
- Cons: Requires Docker, slower, more complex setup

**Approach B: Use the existing CI Postgres with a test schema**
- Create a temporary schema in the CI Postgres, run migrations against that schema, then clean up after
- Pros: No Docker needed in CI, uses existing infra
- Cons: Drizzle doesn't support custom schemas easily, would need hacking

**Approach C: Use the existing local Docker Compose**
- Rely on `docker compose up` having been run, create a separate test database, apply migrations and test, then drop the database
- Pros: Reuses existing infra, clean isolation via separate database
- Cons: Requires Docker to be running, test database creation/cleanup

**Approach D: Lightweight test using the migrate.ts pattern**
- I'd create a test file that spins up a temporary database, runs the migrations through Drizzle's migrate API with a modified journal, inserts test data, runs migration 0003, verifies the schema changes and data integrity, then cleans up
- Pros: Clean, isolated, uses existing Drizzle infrastructure
- Cons: Requires `PG_CONNECTION_STRING` in env

The most practical approach is probably a test script that connects to Postgres, creates a temp database, applies migrations sequentially with verification between steps, tests the specific scenario, and cleans up. But what I really need to verify is that invitations fail before 0003 (missing createdAt) and work after 0003 — this is more of a functional test than a pure schema migration test. The most useful test would apply migrations 0000-0002, insert an invitation row without createdAt like the old schema, then apply 0003 to verify the ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN works correctly.

Now I'm thinking through how to verify that existing invitation rows get populated with the DEFAULT NOW() value and that new rows can include both createdAt and teamId, which proves the migration is backwards-compatible.

1→services:
     2→  postgres:
     3→    image: postgres:16-alpine
     4→    ports:
     5→      - "5432:5432"
     6→    environment:
     7→      POSTGRES_USER: postgres
     8→      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres
     9→      POSTGRES_DB: rudel
    10→    volumes:
    11→      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    12→    healthcheck:
    13→      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U postgres"]
    14→      interval: 5s
    15→      timeout: 3s
    16→      retries: 5
    17→
    18→  clickhouse:
    19→    image: clickhouse/clickhouse-server:latest
    20→    ports:
    21→      - "8123:8123"
    22→    environment:
    23→      CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD: clickhouse
    24→    volumes:
    25→      - clickhouse_data:/var/lib/clickhouse
    26→      - ./scripts/init-clickhouse-local.sql:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init.sql:ro
    27→    healthcheck:
    28→      test: ["CMD", "clickhouse-client", "--password", "clickhouse", "--query", "SELECT 1"]
    29→      interval: 5s
    30→      timeout: 3s
    31→      retries: 5
    32→
    33→volumes:
    34→  postgres_data:
    35→  clickhouse_data:
    36→

content.content:
1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→set -e
     3→
     4→# 1. Start Postgres + ClickHouse (idempotent)
     5→docker compose up -d --wait
     6→
     7→# 2. Run Postgres migrations (idempotent — skips already-applied)
     8→PG_CONNECTION_STRING=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/rudel \
     9→  bun run --cwd packages/sql-schema migrate
    10→
    11→# 3. Start API + Web in parallel with local env vars
    12→export PG_CONNECTION_STRING=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/rudel
    13→export BETTER_AUTH_SECRET=local-dev-secret-that-is-at-least-32-chars-long
    14→export CLICKHOUSE_URL=http://localhost:8123
    15→export REDACTED
    16→export APP_URL=http://localhost:4011
    17→
    18→# Run API and Web in parallel, kill both on Ctrl+C
    19→bun --watch apps/api/src/index.ts &
    20→API_PID=$!
    21→bun run --cwd apps/web dev &
    22→WEB_PID=$!
    23→
    24→trap "kill $API_PID $WEB_PID 2>/dev/null" EXIT
    25→wait
    26→

1→{
     2→	"name": "@rudel/ch-schema",
     3→	"version": "0.0.0",
     4→	"private": true,
     5→	"type": "module",
     6→	"exports": {
     7→		".": "./src/index.ts"
     8→	},
     9→	"scripts": {
    10→		"check-types": "tsc --noEmit",
    11→		"test": "bun test",
    12→		"ch:generate": "doppler run --project rudel --config ci -- bun --bun chkit generate",
    13→		"ch:generate:dryrun": "doppler run --project rudel --config ci -- bun --bun chkit generate --dryrun",
    14→		"ch:migrate": "doppler run --project rudel --config ci -- bash -c 'CLICKHOUSE_DB=default bun --bun chkit migrate --apply'",
    15→		"ch:migrate:prd": "doppler run --project rudel --config prd -- bash -c 'CLICKHOUSE_DB=default bun --bun chkit migrate --apply'",
    16→		"ch:status": "doppler run --project rudel --config ci -- bash -c 'CLICKHOUSE_DB=default bun --bun chkit status'",
    17→		"ch:status:prd": "doppler run --project rudel --config prd -- bash -c 'CLICKHOUSE_DB=default bun --bun chkit status'",
    18→		"ch:codegen": "doppler run --project rudel --config ci -- bun --bun chkit codegen",
    19→		"ch:drift": "doppler run --project rudel --config ci -- bash -c 'CLICKHOUSE_DB=default bun --bun chkit drift'",
    20→		"ch:query": "doppler run --project rudel --config ci -- bash -c 'curl -sk \"${CLICKHOUSE_URL}/?user=${CLICKHOUSE_USERNAME}&password=${CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD}\" --data-binary @\"$1\"' --",
    21→		"ch:describe": "doppler run --project rudel --config ci -- bash -c 'curl -sk \"${CLICKHOUSE_URL}/?user=${CLICKHOUSE_USERNAME}&password=${CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD}\" --data-binary \"DESCRIBE $1 FORMAT Pretty\"' --",
    22→		"chcli": "doppler run --project rudel --config ci -- bash -c 'CLICKHOUSE_HOST=${CLICKHOUSE_URL#https://} CLICKHOUSE_USER=$CLICKHOUSE_USERNAME CLICKHOUSE_SECURE=true CLICKHOUSE_PORT=443 chcli \"$@\"' --",
    23→		"chcli:prd": "doppler run --project rudel --config prd -- bash -c 'CLICKHOUSE_HOST=${CLICKHOUSE_URL#https://} CLICKHOUSE_USER=$CLICKHOUSE_USERNAME CLICKHOUSE_SECURE=true CLICKHOUSE_PORT=443 chcli \"$@\"' --"
    24→	},
    25→	"dependencies": {
    26→		"@chkit/clickhouse": "0.1.0-beta.9",
    27→		"@chkit/codegen": "0.1.0-beta.9",
    28→		"@chkit/core": "0.1.0-beta.9",
    29→		"@chkit/plugin-codegen": "0.1.0-beta.9",
    30→		"@chkit/plugin-obsessiondb": "0.1.0-beta.9",
    31→		"@chkit/plugin-pull": "0.1.0-beta.9",
    32→		"chkit": "0.1.0-beta.9",
    33→		"zod": "^3.24.0"
    34→	},
    35→	"devDependencies": {
    36→		"@obsessiondb/chcli": "^0.1.1-beta.4",
    37→		"@rudel/typescript-config": "workspace:*",
    38→		"bun-types": "latest",
    39→		"typescript": "5.9.2"
    40→	}
    41→}
    42→

1→import { boolean, pgTable, text, timestamp } from "drizzle-orm/pg-core";
     2→
     3→export const user = pgTable("user", {
     4→	id: text("id").primaryKey(),
     5→	name: text("name").notNull(),
     6→	email: text("email").notNull().unique(),
     7→	emailVerified: boolean("email_verified").default(false).notNull(),
     8→	image: text("image"),
     9→	createdAt: timestamp("created_at", { withTimezone: true, mode: "date" })
    10→		.defaultNow()
    11→		.notNull(),
    12→	updatedAt: timestamp("updated_at", { withTimezone: true, mode: "date" })
    13→		.defaultNow()
    14→		.$onUpdate(() => new Date())
    15→		.notNull(),
    16→});
    17→
    18→export const session = pgTable("session", {
    19→	id: text("id").primaryKey(),
    20→	expiresAt: timestamp("expires_at", {
    21→		withTimezone: true,
    22→		mode: "date",
    23→	}).notNull(),
    24→	token: text("token").notNull().unique(),
    25→	createdAt: timestamp("created_at", { withTimezone: true, mode: "date" })
    26→		.defaultNow()
    27→		.notNull(),
    28→	updatedAt: timestamp("updated_at", { withTimezone: true, mode: "date" })
    29→		.defaultNow()
    30→		.$onUpdate(() => new Date())
    31→		.notNull(),
    32→	ipAddress: text("ip_address"),
    33→	userAgent: text("user_agent"),
    34→	userId: text("user_id")
    35→		.notNull()
    36→		.references(() => user.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
    37→	activeOrganizationId: text("active_organization_id"),
    38→});
    39→
    40→export const account = pgTable("account", {
    41→	id: text("id").primaryKey(),
    42→	accountId: text("account_id").notNull(),
    43→	providerId: text("provider_id").notNull(),
    44→	userId: text("user_id")
    45→		.notNull()
    46→		.references(() => user.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
    47→	accessToken: text("access_token"),
    48→	refreshToken: text("refresh_token"),
    49→	idToken: text("id_token"),
    50→	accessTokenExpiresAt: timestamp("access_token_expires_at", {
    51→		withTimezone: true,
    52→		mode: "date",
    53→	}),
    54→	refreshTokenExpiresAt: timestamp("refresh_token_expires_at", {
    55→		withTimezone: true,
    56→		mode: "date",
    57→	}),
    58→	scope: text("scope"),
    59→	password: text("password"),
    60→	createdAt: timestamp("created_at", { withTimezone: true, mode: "date" })
    61→		.defaultNow()
    62→		.notNull(),
    63→	updatedAt: timestamp("updated_at", { withTimezone: true, mode: "date" })
    64→		.defaultNow()
    65→		.$onUpdate(() => new Date())
    66→		.notNull(),
    67→});
    68→
    69→export const verification = pgTable("verification", {
    70→	id: text("id").primaryKey(),
    71→	identifier: text("identifier").notNull(),
    72→	value: text("value").notNull(),
    73→	expiresAt: timestamp("expires_at", {
    74→		withTimezone: true,
    75→		mode: "date",
    76→	}).notNull(),
    77→	createdAt: timestamp("created_at", { withTimezone: true, mode: "date" })
    78→		.defaultNow()
    79→		.notNull(),
    80→	updatedAt: timestamp("updated_at", { withTimezone: true, mode: "date" })
    81→		.defaultNow()
    82→		.$onUpdate(() => new Date())
    83→		.notNull(),
    84→});
    85→
    86→export const organization = pgTable("organization", {
    87→	id: text("id").primaryKey(),
    88→	name: text("name").notNull(),
    89→	slug: text("slug").notNull().unique(),
    90→	logo: text("logo"),
    91→	metadata: text("metadata"),
    92→	createdAt: timestamp("created_at", { withTimezone: true, mode: "date" })
    93→		.defaultNow()
    94→		.notNull(),
    95→});
    96→
    97→export const member = pgTable("member", {
    98→	id: text("id").primaryKey(),
    99→	organizationId: text("organization_id")
   100→		.notNull()
   101→		.references(() => organization.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
   102→	userId: text("user_id")
   103→		.notNull()
   104→		.references(() => user.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
   105→	role: text("role").notNull().default("member"),
   106→	createdAt: timestamp("created_at", { withTimezone: true, mode: "date" })
   107→		.defaultNow()
   108→		.notNull(),
   109→});
   110→
   111→export const invitation = pgTable("invitation", {
   112→	id: text("id").primaryKey(),
   113→	organizationId: text("organization_id")
   114→		.notNull()
   115→		.references(() => organization.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
   116→	email: text("email").notNull(),
   117→	role: text("role"),
   118→	status: text("status").notNull().default("pending"),
   119→	expiresAt: timestamp("expires_at", {
   120→		withTimezone: true,
   121→		mode: "date",
   122→	}).notNull(),
   123→	inviterId: text("inviter_id")
   124→		.notNull()
   125→		.references(() => user.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
   126→	createdAt: timestamp("created_at", { withTimezone: true, mode: "date" })
   127→		.defaultNow()
   128→		.notNull(),
   129→	teamId: text("team_id"),
   130→});
   131→

   133→export type UserInsert = typeof user.$inferInsert;
   134→export type SessionSelect = typeof session.$inferSelect;

content.content:
1→name: CI
     2→
     3→on:
     4→  pull_request:
     5→  push:
     6→    branches:
     7→      - main
     8→
     9→jobs:
    10→  verify:
    11→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    12→    steps:
    13→      - name: Checkout
    14→        uses: actions/checkout@v4
    15→
    16→      - name: Setup Bun
    17→        uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
    18→        with:
    19→          bun-version: 1.3.5
    20→
    21→      - name: Cache bun install
    22→        uses: actions/cache@v4
    23→        with:
    24→          path: ~/.bun/install/cache
    25→          key: ${{ runner.os }}-bun-${{ hashFiles('**/bun.lock') }}
    26→          restore-keys: |
    27→            ${{ runner.os }}-bun-
    28→
    29→      - name: Install dependencies
    30→        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
    31→
    32→      - name: Verify lockfile unchanged
    33→        run: git diff --exit-code bun.lock
    34→
    35→      - name: Verify
    36→        run: bunx turbo run lint check-types test build
    37→        env:
    38→          PG_CONNECTION_STRING: ${{ secrets.PG_CONNECTION_STRING }}
    39→          CLICKHOUSE_URL: ${{ secrets.CLICKHOUSE_URL }}
    40→          CLICKHOUSE_USERNAME: ${{ secrets.CLICKHOUSE_USERNAME }}
    41→          CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD }}
    42→          CLICKHOUSE_DB: ${{ secrets.CLICKHOUSE_DB }}
    43→
    44→  deploy:
    45→    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name == 'push'
    46→    needs: verify
    47→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    48→    steps:
    49→      - name: Checkout
    50→        uses: actions/checkout@v4
    51→
    52→      - name: Setup Fly.io
    53→        uses: superfly/flyctl-actions/setup-flyctl@master
    54→
    55→      - name: Deploy
    56→        run: flyctl deploy --remote-only
    57→        env:
    58→          FLY_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.FLY_API_TOKEN }}
    59→

content.content:
1→-- Local ClickHouse schema for development
     2→-- Derived from packages/ch-schema/chx/migrations/20260220190421_auto.sql
     3→-- Uses ReplacingMergeTree (local) instead of SharedReplacingMergeTree (cloud)
     4→
     5→CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS rudel;
     6→
     7→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS rudel.claude_sessions
     8→(
     9→  `session_date` DateTime64(3, 'UTC') DEFAULT now64(3),
    10→  `last_interaction_date` DateTime64(3, 'UTC') DEFAULT now64(3),
    11→  `session_id` String,
    12→  `organization_id` String,
    13→  `project_path` String,
    14→  `repository` Nullable(String),
    15→  `content` String,
    16→  `subagents` Map(String, String) DEFAULT map(),
    17→  `skills` Array(String) DEFAULT [],
    18→  `slash_commands` Array(String) DEFAULT [],
    19→  `subagent_types` Array(String) DEFAULT [],
    20→  `ingested_at` DateTime64(3, 'UTC') DEFAULT now64(3),
    21→  `user_id` String,
    22→  `git_branch` Nullable(String),
    23→  `git_sha` Nullable(String),
    24→  `input_tokens` UInt64 DEFAULT 0,
    25→  `output_tokens` UInt64 DEFAULT 0,
    26→  `cache_read_input_tokens` UInt64 DEFAULT 0,
    27→  `cache_creation_input_tokens` UInt64 DEFAULT 0,
    28→  `total_tokens` UInt64 DEFAULT 0,
    29→  `tag` Nullable(String)
    30→) ENGINE = ReplacingMergeTree(ingested_at)
    31→PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(toDate(session_date))
    32→ORDER BY (`organization_id`, `session_date`, `session_id`)
    33→TTL toDate(session_date) + toIntervalDay(365)
    34→SETTINGS index_granularity = 8192;
    35→
    36→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS rudel.session_analytics
    37→(
    38→  `session_date` DateTime64(3, 'UTC') DEFAULT now64(3),
    39→  `last_interaction_date` DateTime64(3, 'UTC') DEFAULT now64(3),
    40→  `session_id` String,
    41→  `organization_id` String,
    42→  `project_path` String,
    43→  `repository` Nullable(String),
    44→  `content` String,
    45→  `subagents` Map(String, String) DEFAULT map(),
    46→  `skills` Array(String) DEFAULT [],
    47→  `slash_commands` Array(String) DEFAULT [],
    48→  `subagent_types` Array(String) DEFAULT [],
    49→  `ingested_at` DateTime64(3, 'UTC') DEFAULT now64(3),
    50→  `user_id` String,
    51→  `git_branch` Nullable(String),
    52→  `git_sha` Nullable(String),
    53→  `input_tokens` UInt64 DEFAULT 0,
    54→  `output_tokens` UInt64 DEFAULT 0,
    55→  `cache_read_input_tokens` UInt64 DEFAULT 0,
    56→  `cache_creation_input_tokens` UInt64 DEFAULT 0,
    57→  `total_tokens` UInt64 DEFAULT 0,
    58→  `tag` Nullable(String),
    59→  `total_interactions` UInt32 DEFAULT 0,
    60→  `actual_duration_min` UInt32 DEFAULT 0,
    61→  `avg_period_sec` Float64 DEFAULT 0,
    62→  `median_period_sec` Float64 DEFAULT 0,
    63→  `quick_responses` UInt32 DEFAULT 0,
    64→  `normal_responses` UInt32 DEFAULT 0,
    65→  `long_pauses` UInt32 DEFAULT 0,
    66→  `error_count` UInt32 DEFAULT 0,
    67→  `model_used` String DEFAULT '',
    68→  `has_commit` UInt8 DEFAULT 0,
    69→  `session_archetype` String DEFAULT 'standard',
    70→  `success_score` UInt8 DEFAULT 0,
    71→  `used_plan_mode` UInt8 DEFAULT 0,
    72→  `inference_duration_sec` UInt32 DEFAULT 0,
    73→  `human_duration_sec` UInt32 DEFAULT 0,
    74→  INDEX `idx_model_used` (model_used) TYPE set(0) GRANULARITY 4,
    75→  INDEX `idx_project_path` (project_path) TYPE set(0) GRANULARITY 4,
    76→  INDEX `idx_repository` (repository) TYPE set(0) GRANULARITY 4,
    77→  INDEX `idx_user_id` (user_id) TYPE set(0) GRANULARITY 4
    78→) ENGINE = ReplacingMergeTree(ingested_at)
    79→PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(toDate(session_date))
    80→ORDER BY (`organization_id`, `session_date`, `session_id`)
    81→SETTINGS index_granularity = 8192;
    82→
    83→CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW IF NOT EXISTS rudel.session_analytics_mv TO rudel.session_analytics AS
    84→WITH
    85→  arrayFilter(x -> JSONExtractString(x, 'type') IN ('user', 'assistant'), splitByChar('\n', cs.content)) AS _interaction_lines,
    86→  arrayFilter(x -> JSONHas(x, 'timestamp'), _interaction_lines) AS _ts_lines,
    87→  arrayMap(x -> parseDateTime64BestEffort(JSONExtractString(x, 'timestamp')), _ts_lines) AS _timestamps,
    88→  arrayMap(x -> JSONExtractString(x, 'type'), _ts_lines) AS _msg_types,
    89→  if(length(_timestamps) > 1, arrayMap(i -> dateDiff('second', _timestamps[i], _timestamps[i+1]), range(1, length(_timestamps))), []) AS _prompt_periods_sec,
    90→  if(length(_timestamps) > 1, arrayMap(i -> if(_msg_types[i] = 'user' AND _msg_types[i+1] = 'assistant', dateDiff('second', _timestamps[i], _timestamps[i+1]), 0), range(1, length(_timestamps))), []) AS _inference_gaps,
    91→  if(length(_timestamps) > 1, arrayMap(i -> if(_msg_types[i] = 'assistant' AND _msg_types[i+1] = 'user', dateDiff('second', _timestamps[i], _timestamps[i+1]), 0), range(1, length(_timestamps))), []) AS _human_gaps
    92→SELECT *,
    93→  toUInt32(length(_timestamps)) as total_interactions,
    94→  toUInt32(dateDiff('minute', arrayMin(_timestamps), arrayMax(_timestamps))) as actual_duration_min,
    95→  if(length(_prompt_periods_sec) > 0, round(arrayAvg(_prompt_periods_sec), 2), 0) as avg_period_sec,
    96→  if(length(_prompt_periods_sec) > 0, toFloat64(arrayElement(arraySort(_prompt_periods_sec), toUInt64(ceil(length(_prompt_periods_sec) / 2)))), 0) as median_period_sec,
    97→  toUInt32(arrayCount(x -> x < 5, _prompt_periods_sec)) as quick_responses,
    98→  toUInt32(arrayCount(x -> x >= 5 AND x <= 60, _prompt_periods_sec)) as normal_responses,
    99→  toUInt32(arrayCount(x -> x > 300, _prompt_periods_sec)) as long_pauses,
   100→  toUInt32(length(extractAll(cs.content, '"isApiErrorMessage":true')) + length(extractAll(cs.content, '"is_error":true'))) as error_count,
   101→  JSONExtractString(JSONExtractRaw(arrayElement(arrayFilter(x -> JSONExtractString(x, 'type') = 'assistant', splitByChar('\n', cs.content)), -1), 'message'), 'model') as model_used,
   102→  toUInt8(if(cs.git_sha IS NOT NULL AND cs.git_sha != '', 1, 0)) as has_commit,
   103→  toUInt8(if(position(cs.content, '"name":"EnterPlanMode"') > 0, 1, 0)) as used_plan_mode,
   104→  toUInt32(arraySum(_inference_gaps)) as inference_duration_sec,
   105→  toUInt32(arraySum(_human_gaps)) as human_duration_sec,
   106→  CASE
   107→    WHEN dateDiff('minute', cs.session_date, cs.last_interaction_date) <= 10 AND cs.total_tokens < 500000 AND cs.output_tokens > 1000 THEN 'quick_win'
   108→    WHEN dateDiff('minute', cs.session_date, cs.last_interaction_date) > 30 AND cs.output_tokens > 50000 AND cs.git_sha IS NOT NULL AND cs.git_sha != '' THEN 'deep_work'
   109→    WHEN cs.total_tokens > 1000000 AND (cs.output_tokens / nullif(cs.input_tokens, 0)) < 0.3 AND dateDiff('minute', cs.session_date, cs.last_interaction_date) > 20 THEN 'struggle'
   110→    WHEN length(cs.skills) >= 3 AND (cs.git_sha IS NULL OR cs.git_sha = '') AND cs.total_tokens > 200000 THEN 'exploration'
   111→    WHEN dateDiff('minute', cs.session_date, cs.last_interaction_date) < 3 AND cs.output_tokens < 500 THEN 'abandoned'
   112→    ELSE 'standard'
   113→  END as session_archetype,
   114→  toUInt8(round(
   115→    50
   116→    + (if(cs.git_sha IS NOT NULL AND cs.git_sha != '', 20, 0))
   117→    + (if((cs.output_tokens / nullif(cs.input_tokens, 0)) > 0.5, 15, 0))
   118→    + (least(toUInt32(length(cs.skills)), 3) * 5)
   119→    - (if(cs.total_tokens > 1500000 AND (cs.git_sha IS NULL OR cs.git_sha = ''), 20, 0))
   120→    - (if(dateDiff('minute', cs.session_date, cs.last_interaction_date) < 2 AND cs.output_tokens < 200, 30, 0))
   121→    - (least(toUInt32(length(extractAll(cs.content, '"isApiErrorMessage":true')) + length(extractAll(cs.content, '"is_error":true'))), 10) * 2)
   122→  )) as success_score
   123→FROM rudel.claude_sessions AS cs
   124→WHERE length(_timestamps) > 0
   125→QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY cs.session_id ORDER BY cs.ingested_at DESC) = 1;
   126→

1→-- Create a personal organization for each existing user (id = user.id so ClickHouse data maps without changes)
     2→INSERT INTO "organization" ("id", "name", "slug", "created_at")
     3→SELECT "id", "name" || '''s Workspace', lower(regexp_replace(split_part("email", '@', 1), '[^a-z0-9-]', '-', 'g')) || '-' || substr("id", 1, 8), now()
     4→FROM "user"
     5→ON CONFLICT ("id") DO NOTHING;
     6→--> statement-breakpoint
     7→-- Create owner membership for each user in their personal org
     8→INSERT INTO "member" ("id", "organization_id", "user_id", "role", "created_at")
     9→SELECT gen_random_uuid()::text, u."id", u."id", 'owner', now()
    10→FROM "user" u
    11→WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    12→  SELECT 1 FROM "member" m WHERE m."user_id" = u."id" AND m."organization_id" = u."id"
    13→);
    14→--> statement-breakpoint
    15→-- Set active org on existing sessions
    16→UPDATE "session" SET "active_organization_id" = "user_id"
    17→WHERE "active_organization_id" IS NULL;
    18→
